name: Custom Python Sensor
description: A template for creating custom HoneyWire sensors in Python.

documentation.summary: This is a blueprint for writing your own HoneyWire sensors in Python. It provides the base structure to integrate with the Python SDK and report events securely to the Hub.

sections.title: Features
content: Easy SDK Integration: Automatically hooks into the event bus.
Custom Detection: Write any Python logic to trigger events.

sections.title: Security Architecture
content: Unprivileged Execution: Runs completely as non-root user.
Kernel Capability Stripping: Drops ALL Linux kernel capabilities.
Read-Only Context: Enforced via the Hub's deployment engine.

env_vars.name: HW_HUB_ENDPOINT
env_vars.description: The URL of your central HoneyWire Hub.

env_vars.name: HW_SENSOR_ID
env_vars.description: Unique identifier for this specific trap.

env_vars.name: HW_CUSTOM_TARGET
env_vars.description: Example custom variable injected into the Python runtime.
